---
title: "Teaching"
title-block-style: none
pagetitle: "Teaching | Mesfin Genie"
description: "Teaching portfolio and public behavioural economics resources."
page-layout: full
image: /assets/img/og-card.png
---

```{=html}
<header class="page-header"><div class="page-shell"><p class="eyebrow">Teaching</p><h1>Health economics and behavioural economics</h1><p class="lead">Course coordination, teaching and selected public learning resources at The University of Newcastle.</p></div></header>
<section class="content-section"><div class="page-shell"><h2>Courses</h2><ul class="course-list"><li><div class="meta">ECON3111 · 2025–2026</div><h3>Behavioural Economics</h3><p><strong>Lecturer and Course Coordinator / Primary Course Coordinator.</strong> Rational-choice benchmarks, behavioural mechanisms, decision-making under risk and uncertainty, experiments and behavioural public policy.</p></li><li><div class="meta">ECON2112 · 2025–2026</div><h3>Health Economics and Finance</h3><p><strong>Lecturer and Course Coordinator / Primary Course Coordinator.</strong> Healthcare markets, provider and patient behaviour, public and private finance, economic evaluation and health-policy decisions.</p></li><li><div class="meta">ECON3011 · 2024</div><h3>Introduction to Health Economics and Finance</h3><p><strong>Primary Course Coordinator.</strong> Foundations of health economics, health financing, healthcare demand, insurance and introductory economic evaluation.</p></li></ul></div></section>
<section class="content-section"><div class="page-shell"><div class="section-heading"><div><p class="eyebrow">Open resources</p><h2>Behavioural economics</h2></div><a class="button-text" href="/teaching/behavioural-economics/">All resources →</a></div><div class="resource-row"><img src="/assets/teaching/week1-foundations.webp" width="900" height="506" loading="lazy" alt="Preview of Week 1 behavioural economics teaching material"><div><h3>Foundations and rational choice under certainty</h3><p>Preferences, utility representation, feasible choice sets, rational-choice benchmarks and the distinction between normative and descriptive analysis.</p><p><a href="/blog/posts/the-benchmark-before-bias/">Read the related explainer →</a></p></div></div><div class="resource-row"><img src="/assets/teaching/week6-risk-uncertainty.webp" width="900" height="506" loading="lazy" alt="Preview of Week 6 behavioural economics teaching material"><div><h3>Advanced choice under risk and uncertainty</h3><p>Expected utility, independence, the Allais and Ellsberg paradoxes, probability weighting, prospect theory, loss aversion and ambiguity.</p><p><a href="/blog/posts/why-99-does-not-feel-like-100/">Read the related explainer →</a></p></div></div></div></section>
```
